title: Screen recording (⌘⇧5): region/full, audio device, floating stop, into history
goal: TOP-PRIORITY per the user: screen-video capture is one of the most important features. Match CleanShot's ⌘⇧5. Use ScreenCaptureKit (SCStream) — the modern macOS path for screen + audio, not the deprecated AVCaptureScreenInput. Must let the user pick the SCREEN REGION (full/region/window), choose AUDIO INPUTS (system and/or mic), and record to MP4 plus GIF and animated WebP. Recordings land in the same history bin as screenshots (phase 11). Recording session/config logic lives in a testable core type; AVAssetWriter/SCStream plumbing is app-side.

tasks.title: ⌘⇧5 recording entry: choose whole screen or a region
tasks.notes: Global ⌘⇧5 starts the record flow with a selector for full display vs a dragged region (reuse the region-capture overlay from capture). Wire through the menu-bar agent. Establish the SCStream + AVAssetWriter pipeline writing an .mov/.mp4 to a temp file.

tasks.title: Audio device picker (system audio and/or microphone)
tasks.notes: Before/at record start, let the user pick audio source(s): system audio (SCStream audio), a microphone (AVCaptureDevice), or none. Persist the last choice. Mux audio into the recording via AVAssetWriter.

tasks.title: Floating stop control that is excluded from the recording
tasks.notes: Show a small always-on-top stop button (and elapsed time) in a screen corner during recording. It must NOT appear in the captured video — exclude its window from the SCStream content filter (SCContentFilter excludingWindows). Stopping finalizes the asset.

tasks.title: Recordings drop into the history bin alongside images
tasks.notes: Extend CaptureHistory/CaptureStore (TDD) to hold video entries (duration, file URL, a poster-frame thumbnail) next to image entries. The slide-down bin (phase 11) and Quick Access Overlay (11.5) show video items with a play affordance; Copy puts the file on the clipboard, Edit opens the video editor (phase 13).

tasks.title: Record to MP4 + GIF + animated WebP
tasks.notes: User wants recording directly to GIF and WebP, not just MP4. Offer the output format at record start (or as a quick convert from the recording): H.264 MP4 via AVAssetWriter; animated GIF via CGImageDestination (kCGImagePropertyGIFDictionary) with fps/size caps; animated WebP (CGImageDestination supports WebP on macOS 26 — verify, else fall back to a frame-encode path). Shares the trim/crop pipeline with phase 13 so you can record → trim → export in the chosen format.
